# Contributing to HappyBirthday 🎉

Thank you for your interest in contributing! Please follow the steps below to ensure a smooth collaboration experience.

---

## 🌿 Branch Naming Convention

**Every contributor must create a branch named after themselves.** This keeps contributions organized and easy to trace.

```
your-name
```

**Examples:**
- `john-doe`
- `sarah-smith`
- `alex-dev`

### How to create your branch:

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone git@github.com:ovishkh/HappyBirthday.git
cd HappyBirthday

# Create a branch with YOUR name
git checkout -b your-name

# Make your changes, then push
git push origin your-name
```

---

## 📥 Creating a Pull Request

After pushing your branch, open a **detailed Pull Request** on GitHub. A good PR helps reviewers understand your changes quickly.

### Your PR must include:

1. **A clear title** — describe what your change does in a few words
   - ✅ `Add new birthday theme - ocean vibes`
   - ❌ `fix stuff`

2. **A detailed description** with:
   - What you changed and why
   - Screenshots or recordings (for UI changes)
   - Any related issues (use `Closes #<issue-number>` to link them)

3. **Checklist** — make sure you've:
   - [ ] Tested your changes locally
   - [ ] Made sure no files are broken
   - [ ] Followed the branch naming convention (`your-name`)

### PR Template example:

```markdown
## What did I change?
Describe the changes you made clearly.

## Why?
Explain the reason or motivation behind this change.

## Screenshots (if applicable)
Add before/after screenshots here.

## Related Issues
Closes #<issue-number>
```

---

## ✅ Contribution Checklist

- [ ] Branch is named after you (`your-name`)
- [ ] Changes are well-tested locally
- [ ] PR has a descriptive title and detailed description
- [ ] No unrelated files included in the commit

---

## 💬 Need Help?

Open an [issue](https://github.com/ovishkh/HappyBirthday/issues) and describe what you need. We're happy to help!
